text:
The lack of a dedicated impact factor for Polish journals in the humanities and social sciences (HSS) has long necessitated a more nuanced approach to academic evaluation. This paper details the development and implementation of POL-index, a national citation database designed to quantify the scholarly impact of Polish HSS journals. Unlike traditional bibliometric tools, POL-index addresses field-specific challenges, such as longer citation half-lives and unique citation structures, by employing a flexible data acquisition model that integrates publisher submissions with automated XML harvesting. 

The core of the system is the Polish Impact Factor (PWW), a metric specifically engineered for longitudinal stability and disciplinary relevance. The proposed formula balances the need for current performance data with the reality of HSS citation patterns by analyzing a five-year citation window while emphasizing the most recent two years of activity. The paper outlines the technical architecture of the platform—including its citation identification engine and verification modules—and discusses the strategic roadmap for evolving the system from internal data entry to an automated ecosystem that incorporates monographs and diverse bibliographic sources. Ultimately, POL-index aims to provide a transparent, evidence-based foundation for the ministerial evaluation of Polish scholarly journals.
